fields.description:
## Why
`CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` is exposed as an env var ONLY in the remote/cloud runner; the LOCAL
Claude Code CLI does not export it. Without a shim the session-provenance feature silently
no-ops for local-CLI users — the most common case. The robust, harness-agnostic capture is a
Claude Code SessionStart hook that reads the hook's `session_id` from stdin and exports it as
the portable `REBAR_SESSION_ID`, plus the harness tag `AI_AGENT=claude-code`.

## What
- **Hook script (`scripts/session-shims/claude-code-session-start.sh`).** Reads the
  SessionStart JSON envelope from stdin (field `session_id`) and, when `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` is
  set, APPENDS `export REBAR_SESSION_ID=<id>` and `export AI_AGENT=claude-code` to it. These
  are the Claude Code hook-contract facts — the SessionStart stdin `session_id` field and the
  `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` env-injection file — per the **Claude Code hooks reference**
  (https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks), cited in the script header + docs page. GRACEFUL
  DEGRADATION: this is the only external-API coupling, and if a future Claude Code renames the
  field/var or changes the mechanism, the shim simply no-ops (no `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` / unparsed
  `session_id` → exit 0) — it never errors or blocks the session, so a wrong assumption fails
  safe. The harness tag is the base name `claude-code` (the `AI_AGENT` vocabulary permits an
  optional `_<version>` suffix, but the local hook has no reliable version source, so it emits
  the bare base name). Uses `python3` (already a rebar dependency) to parse the JSON
  robustly — any parse failure, a missing/blank `session_id`, or an absent `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` is
  a silent no-op with exit 0 (fire-and-forget; NEVER blocks the session). Idempotent: re-firing
  on resume/clear/compact first STRIPS this hook's own prior `REBAR_SESSION_ID`/`AI_AGENT`
  exports from the env file (preserving other hooks' lines) then re-writes the current values,
  so the file never accumulates duplicate/stale lines and always reflects the CURRENT session
  (whose id may differ across resume per gotcha G1 — not "the same value").
- **Vocabulary alignment.** Update the `claude-code_<ver>` examples in `docs/config.md`,
  `docs/event-schema.md`, and the `session_id.py` comment to present the harness tag as a base
  name with an OPTIONAL `_<version>` suffix, so bare `claude-code` is consistent with the
  documented vocabulary.
- **Docs (`docs/session-id-shims.md`).** A new install guide: the `settings.json`
  `hooks.SessionStart` snippet pointing at the script, the caveat that the id is NOT stable
  across `--resume`/`--continue`/fork/subagents (epic gotcha G1 — treat it as "the session
  that emitted the claim event"), and guidance to keep it session-scoped (do NOT export
  `REBAR_SESSION_ID` from a shell profile — gotcha G3). Cross-links `docs/config.md`.

## Scope
- IN: the Claude Code SessionStart hook script, the `docs/session-id-shims.md` install page,
  the small vocabulary-alignment doc/comment edits (`docs/config.md`, `docs/event-schema.md`,
  `session_id.py`), and a unit test validating the script (valid shell + env-file output for
  mock stdin, version, malformed, and no-op cases).
- OUT: Codex/Cursor shims (S6 / 7656, which extend the same docs page); the record/read core
  (S1-S3, S5).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `scripts/session-shims/claude-code-session-start.sh` exists and is valid shell. Proof:
      `bash -n scripts/session-shims/claude-code-session-start.sh` (asserted in
      `tests/unit/test_session_shims.py`).
- [ ] Given stdin `{"session_id":"abc123"}` and a `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` temp path, the script
      writes `export REBAR_SESSION_ID=abc123` AND `export AI_AGENT=claude-code` to that file.
      Proof: `pytest tests/unit/test_session_shims.py -k claude_code_exports -q`.
- [ ] Robust no-op (exit 0, empty env file, never blocks the session) for each of: a
      blank/missing `session_id`, MALFORMED/unparseable JSON on stdin, and an unset
      `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE`. Proof: `pytest tests/unit/test_session_shims.py -k "noop or malformed" -q`.
- [ ] Idempotent re-fire: firing twice with DIFFERENT `session_id`s leaves exactly ONE
      `REBAR_SESSION_ID` export (the latest), one `AI_AGENT` export, and preserves a pre-existing
      unrelated `export OTHER_HOOK_VAR=…` line (no accumulation, no stale id). Proof:
      `pytest tests/unit/test_session_shims.py -k idempotent -q`.
- [ ] `docs/session-id-shims.md` documents the hook script path + the `settings.json` install
      snippet, and the two caveats by their specific phrasing. Proof: a doc-content assert in
      `test_session_shims.py` checking `SessionStart`,
      `scripts/session-shims/claude-code-session-start.sh`, the `"hooks"`/`"SessionStart"` JSON
      snippet, and (whitespace-normalised) the exact G1 phrase "NOT stable across `--resume` /
      `--continue` / fork / subagents" and the exact G3 phrase "do NOT `export REBAR_SESSION_ID=…`
      from a shell profile".
- [ ] The `claude-code_<ver>` vocabulary examples in `docs/config.md`, `docs/event-schema.md`,
      and `session_id.py` are updated to a base-name + optional `_<version>` form. Proof:
      `pytest tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py -k docs -q` asserts `docs/config.md` NO
      longer contains the old ``claude-code_<ver>` / `opencode`` slash-form AND DOES contain
      ``base name `claude-code```.

## Depends on
- S1 (the `REBAR_SESSION_ID` contract) and S5 (the `AI_AGENT` -> `claim_harness` contract).
